memes.label: Table of Contents
memes.description: 'Complete table of contents for The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling'

memes.label: Appearance
memes.description: 'A central epistemological theme in Tom Jones — the gap between how things appear and what they are, and the moral consequences of judging by surfaces.'

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s novel, beauty serves as an unreliable signifier: physical attractiveness may indicate virtue (Sophia) or mask corruption (Lady Bellaston), forcing both characters and readers to look beyond appearances'

memes.label: Black George
memes.description: "The gamekeeper on Squire Allworthy's estate, father of Molly Seagrim, whose poaching offences and family hardships become an early test of Tom Jones's generosity and loyalty."

memes.label: Bridget Allworthy
memes.description: 'The sister of Squire Allworthy and the secret mother of Tom Jones, whose concealed pregnancy and subsequent deception drive the central mystery of the novel.'

memes.label: Captain Blifil
memes.description: 'The father of Master Blifil, who married Bridget Allworthy for her fortune, treated his own brother with cruel ingratitude, and died of apoplexy shortly after his son''s birth.'

memes.label: Captain Waters
memes.description: 'A captain in the British army whose wife, Mrs Waters, becomes entangled with Tom Jones at the inn at Upton and is later revealed to be Jenny Jones.'

memes.label: Compassion
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s moral vision, compassion — expressed as active benevolence toward the distressed — is the defining mark of a good character, distinguishing true virtue from mere doctrinal correctness'

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: 'In Tom Jones, courage is presented as an ambivalent virtue — admirable when united with benevolence and judgment, but dangerous when it becomes mere rashness or serves vicious ends'

memes.label: Dinner
memes.description: "A recurring social event in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Dinner scenes serve as key dramatic settings where characters clash, plots advance, and Fielding's social satire reaches its sharpest pitch."

memes.label: Discovery
memes.description: "A key narrative mechanism in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Discoveries and revelations drive the plot forward, from the finding of the infant Tom to the climactic revelation of his true identity."

memes.label: Ensign Northerton
memes.description: "A military ensign who insults Sophia Western at an inn, assaults Tom Jones with a bottle, and later escapes custody under mysterious circumstances."

memes.label: Fortune
memes.description: "A central thematic concept in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Fortune represents both chance and providence—the unpredictable forces that govern human affairs and test character through adversity and prosperity alike."

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: 'A central social bond in Henry Fielding''s Tom Jones, encompassing both sincere affection and the hazards of false or self-interested friendship.'

memes.label: Gratitude
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s moral economy, gratitude is the proper response to benevolence received, and its absence — ingratitude — is treated as one of the most damning moral failures a character can commit'

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: "A central moral concept in Henry Fielding's Tom Jones, encompassing both the aristocratic code of reputation and dueling, and a deeper Christian virtue of integrity."

memes.label: Inn
memes.description: "A recurring setting in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Inns serve as the crossroads of eighteenth-century English society, where travelers of all classes meet, conflicts erupt, and the picaresque plot unfolds."

memes.label: Jenny Jones
memes.description: 'A servant girl of uncommon learning who is falsely accused of being the mother of the foundling Tom Jones, and who later reappears under the name Mrs Waters.'

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: 'Fielding examines justice as both a legal process and a moral quality, contrasting the fallibility of human courts with the providential justice that ultimately rewards virtue and exposes vice in the novel''s resolution'

memes.label: Lady Bellaston
memes.description: 'A fashionable London noblewoman and distant relation of Sophia Western, who becomes infatuated with Tom Jones and schemes to keep him from Sophia.'

memes.label: Liberty
memes.description: 'A recurring theme in Tom Jones, encompassing personal freedom, the right to choose in marriage, and the political liberty prized by Fielding''s age.'

memes.label: London
memes.description: "The urban setting for the final books of Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). London represents sophistication, corruption, and moral complexity in contrast to the rural simplicity of Somersetshire."

memes.label: Lord Fellamar
memes.description: 'A young London nobleman who falls in love with Sophia Western and becomes a pawn in Lady Bellaston''s schemes against Tom Jones.'

memes.label: Love
memes.description: "One of the central thematic concepts in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Love in its various forms—romantic, filial, charitable—drives the novel's plot and serves as the foundation of Fielding's moral philosophy."

memes.label: Man of the Hill
memes.description: "A mysterious hermit encountered by Tom Jones and Partridge on a hill near the Welsh border, who recounts his life story across three chapters and engages Jones in a philosophical debate about human nature and Providence."

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: 'In Tom Jones, marriage is examined as both a social institution and a moral choice, with Fielding distinguishing unions founded on love from those contracted for fortune, lust, or convenience'

memes.label: Master Blifil
memes.description: "The hypocritical nephew of Squire Allworthy and antagonist to Tom Jones in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Outwardly pious and dutiful, Blifil's concealed malice drives the novel's central injustice: Tom's banishment from Paradise Hall."

memes.label: Molly Seagrim
memes.description: "The daughter of Black George the gamekeeper, Molly is Tom Jones's first lover and the mother of his illegitimate child; her bold, combative spirit makes her one of the novel's most vivid lower-class characters."

memes.label: Money
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s novel, money functions as both a plot mechanism and a moral test, revealing character through attitudes toward wealth, from the generosity of Allworthy to the venality of the London elite'

memes.label: Mr Dowling
memes.description: "A Salisbury attorney who becomes a confidant of Blifil and steward to Squire Allworthy, and whose conversation with Tom Jones at an inn reveals the depth of the slander that led to Jones's disinheritance."

memes.label: Mr Fitzpatrick
memes.description: "An Irish gentleman who married Sophia Western's cousin Harriet Fitzpatrick, squandered her fortune, and whose jealous pursuit of his wife leads to a violent confrontation with Tom Jones at the Upton inn."

memes.label: Mr Square
memes.description: 'A philosopher and one of the two tutors engaged by Squire Allworthy to educate Tom Jones and Master Blifil, representing the rationalist school of thought.'

memes.label: Mr Summer
memes.description: "The biological father of Tom Jones, a handsome and accomplished young gentleman educated by Squire Allworthy, who died of small-pox before his son was born."

memes.label: Mr Thwackum
memes.description: 'A clergyman and one of the two tutors at Paradise Hall, representing the rigidly religious pole of the educational debate in Tom Jones.'

memes.label: Mr Watson
memes.description: 'A university acquaintance of the Man of the Hill who leads him into gaming, betrays him to royal troops after the Monmouth Rebellion, and later attempts suicide at Bath before being rescued.'

memes.label: Mrs Abigail
memes.description: "Sophia Western's waiting-woman after the departure of Mrs Honour; a fastidious and sharp-tongued servant whose name Fielding uses as a generic term for ladies' maids."

memes.label: Mrs Deborah Wilkins
memes.description: "The elderly housekeeper at Paradise Hall whose investigation of the foundling in Squire Allworthy's bed sets the parentage mystery in motion."

memes.label: Mrs Etoff
memes.description: "Lady Bellaston's waiting-woman who gathers intelligence about Tom Jones and relays it to her mistress, fueling Lady Bellaston's predatory interest in the handsome foundling."

memes.label: Mrs Fitzpatrick
memes.description: "Sophia Western's cousin, who flees her cruel husband Mr Fitzpatrick and accompanies Sophia on the road to London, where she becomes entangled in Lady Bellaston's schemes."

memes.label: Mrs Honour
memes.description: 'Sophia Western''s loyal but garrulous maid, who serves as an intermediary between Jones and Sophia and provides comic relief throughout the novel.'

memes.label: Mrs Hunt
memes.description: "A wealthy young widow who proposes marriage to Tom Jones, offering her fortune, and whose letter tests Jones's fidelity to Sophia Western."

memes.label: Mrs Miller
memes.description: 'The kind-hearted landlady of the London lodging-house where Tom Jones and Partridge reside, and mother of Nancy and Betty Miller.'

memes.label: Mrs Partridge
memes.description: "The jealous and violent wife of the schoolmaster Partridge, whose fury over her husband's suspected liaison with Jenny Jones sets in motion the chain of events leading to Tom Jones being branded a foundling."

memes.label: Mrs Waters
memes.description: 'The woman rescued by Tom Jones from assault at Upton, who is later revealed to be Jenny Jones — the servant originally accused of being Jones''s mother.'

memes.label: Mrs Western
memes.description: "Squire Western's sister and Sophia's aunt, a woman of pretensions to learning and worldly knowledge who attempts to manage her niece's marital affairs with disastrous results."

memes.label: Mrs Whitefield
memes.description: "The good-natured landlady of the Bell inn at Gloucester, sister of the Methodist preacher George Whitefield, who hosts both Tom Jones and Sophia Western on their respective journeys."

memes.label: Nancy Miller
memes.description: "The elder daughter of Mrs Miller, a virtuous and good-natured young woman whose seduction by Mr Nightingale and eventual marriage to him is one of the novel's key moral episodes."

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: "A foundational concept in Tom Jones, denoting both the innate moral disposition of characters and the natural order against which Fielding measures human conduct."

memes.label: Mr Nightingale
memes.description: "A young gentleman who lodges at Mrs Miller's house in London, becomes a close friend of Tom Jones, and ultimately marries Nancy Miller after Jones persuades him to act honourably."

memes.label: Parson Supple
memes.description: 'The good-natured curate of Squire Allworthy''s parish, notable for his enormous appetite, taciturnity at table, and physical strength.'

memes.label: Partridge
memes.description: "The loyal but cowardly companion of Tom Jones, a former schoolmaster and barber-surgeon whose life is ruined by false accusations of being Tom's father. His superstitious nature, Latin pedantry, and unwavering devotion make him one of Fielding's most memorable comic characters."

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: "In Fielding's Tom Jones, passion denotes the powerful emotional forces—love, anger, desire—that drive human action, requiring the governance of reason and virtue."

memes.label: Reason
memes.description: "In Fielding's Tom Jones, reason is the faculty of moral judgment that must govern the passions—a central Enlightenment value that the novel consistently upholds while exposing its frequent misuse."

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s Tom Jones, religion serves as the moral framework against which characters are measured, distinguishing genuine Christian benevolence from hypocritical performance of piety'

memes.label: Reputation
memes.description: 'A contested social currency in Tom Jones, distinguished by Fielding from true virtue while acknowledged as essential to worldly standing.'

memes.label: Sentiment
memes.description: 'A key term in Fielding''s moral vocabulary, denoting both genuine emotional response and, in its degraded form, affectation or hypocrisy.'

memes.label: Sir George Gresham
memes.description: "A wealthy libertine at Oxford whose extravagant lifestyle and corrupting influence ruin the young narrator in the Man of the Hill's interpolated tale within Fielding's Tom Jones."

memes.label: Sophia Western
memes.description: "The heroine of Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). Daughter of Squire Western, she is celebrated for her beauty, virtue, and constancy in love. Her pursuit of Tom Jones to London forms the romantic heart of the novel."

memes.label: Squire Allworthy
memes.description: "A wealthy and virtuous gentleman of Somersetshire, owner of one of the largest estates in the county, and the adoptive father of Tom Jones. His benevolent heart and sound judgment make him the moral center of Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749)."

memes.label: Squire Western
memes.description: "The boisterous, hard-drinking, fox-hunting father of Sophia Western in Henry Fielding's *The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling* (1749). His tyrannical insistence on controlling his daughter's marriage provides much of the novel's conflict and comedy."

memes.label: Susan Chambermaid
memes.description: 'The robust chambermaid at the inn at Upton, whose bribery by multiple parties and formidable physical prowess make her a memorable minor character in the middle books of the novel.'

memes.label: Tom Jones
memes.description: 'The protagonist and title character of Henry Fielding''s novel, a foundling of unknown parentage raised by the benevolent Squire Allworthy, whose journey from rural Somerset to London tests and ultimately vindicates his innate virtue'

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: "A governing value in Tom Jones, operating at both the narrative level—the narrator's professed commitment to historical truth—and the moral level, where truthfulness distinguishes virtuous from vicious characters."

memes.label: Vice
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s moral universe, vice denotes not merely individual wrongdoing but a systematic disposition toward selfish action disguised by outward propriety, embodied most fully in the character of Master Blifil'

memes.label: Virtue
memes.description: 'In Fielding''s moral philosophy, virtue is understood as active benevolence rooted in good nature rather than abstract principle or doctrinal conformity, embodied in the spontaneous generosity of Tom Jones and the reflective goodness of Squire Allworthy'
